home.hero_description: An open-source web and product analytics platform that combines the power of Mixpanel with the ease of Plausible and one of the best Google Analytics replacements.
home.pricing_event_prompt: Choose how many events you'll track this month
home.pricing_description: Pay only for what you use. Choose your event volume - everything else is unlimited. No surprises, no hidden fees.
home.faq_description: Some of the most common questions we get asked.
home.cta_description: Join thousands of companies using OpenPanel. Free 30-day trial, no credit card required. Self-host for free or use our cloud.
home.sdks_description: Integrate OpenPanel with your favorite framework using our lightweight SDKs. A few lines of code and you're tracking.
home.mcp_description: Connect Claude, Cursor, Windsurf, or your own AI agent to your OpenPanel data via MCP. No more tab-switching or building charts, your AI has the answers.
home.insights_title: Everything you need to understand your users
home.insights_description: From first page view to long-term retention — every touchpoint in one platform. No sampling, no data limits, no guesswork.
home.insights_web_description: Understand your website performance with privacy-first analytics. Track visitors, referrers, and page views without touching user cookies.
home.insights_product_description: Go beyond page views. Track custom events, understand user flows, and explore exactly how people use your product.
home.insights_retention_description: Know how many users come back after day 1, day 7, day 30. Identify which behaviors predict long-term retention.
home.insights_replay_description: Watch real user sessions to see exactly what happened — clicks, scrolls, rage clicks. Privacy controls built in.
home.insights_notifications_description: Get notified when a funnel is completed. Stay on top of key moments in your product without watching dashboards all day.
home.growth_title: Built for teams who ship and measure
home.growth_description: OpenPanel goes beyond page views. Track revenue, monitor conversions, and connect your SEO data — all without switching tools.
home.growth_revenue_description: Connect payment events to track MRR and see which referrers drive the most revenue.
home.growth_conversion_description: Monitor conversion rates over time and break down by A/B variant, country, or device. Catch regressions before they cost you.
home.growth_gsc_description: See which search queries bring organic traffic and how visitors convert after landing. Your SEO and product data, in one place.
home.privacy_description: OpenPanel gives you analytics on your terms - privacy-friendly, open-source, and fully self-hostable. Every part of the platform is designed to put you in control of your data while delivering fast, reliable insights without compromising user trust.
home.privacy_gdpr_description: GDPR compliant and privacy-friendly analytics without cookies or invasive tracking. Data is EU hosted, and a Data Processing Agreement (DPA) is available to sign.
home.privacy_ownership_description: You own your data - no vendors, no sharing, no hidden processing. Store analytics on your own infrastructure and stay in full control.
home.privacy_open_source_description: Full transparency. Audit the code, contribute, fork it, or self-host without lock-in.
home.privacy_self_hosting_description: Deploy OpenPanel anywhere - your server, your cloud, or locally. Full flexibility.
home.privacy_lightweight_description: A tiny, high-performance tracker that won't slow down your site.
home.mixpanel_title: The Best Mixpanel Alternatives in 2026 — Why Teams Switch to OpenPanel
home.mixpanel_description: Looking for the best Mixpanel alternatives in 2026? OpenPanel covers the product analytics features teams actually use — events, funnels, retention, cohorts, and user profiles — without Mixpanel's pricing, privacy trade-offs, or vendor lock-in.
home.mixpanel_cost_description: Mixpanel's pricing scales to hundreds or thousands per month as your event volume grows. OpenPanel starts at $2.50/month — or self-host for free with no event limits.
home.mixpanel_features_description: Events, funnels, retention, cohorts, user profiles, custom dashboards, and A/B testing — all there. OpenPanel covers every core analytics workflow from Mixpanel without the learning curve.
home.mixpanel_self_host_description: Mixpanel is cloud-only. OpenPanel runs on your own infrastructure with a simple Docker setup. Full data ownership, zero vendor lock-in.
home.mixpanel_transparent_description: Mixpanel is a black box. OpenPanel's code is public on GitHub — audit it, contribute to it, or fork it. No surprises, no hidden data processing.
home.testimonials_description: From indie hackers to global teams, OpenPanel helps people understand their users effortlessly.
home.collaboration_description: Build interactive dashboards, share insights with your team, and make data-driven decisions faster. OpenPanel helps you understand not just what's happening, but why.
home.collaboration_features_data_visualizatio: Build line charts, bar charts, sankey flows, and custom dashboards. Combine metrics from any event into a single view.
home.collaboration_features_share_collaborate: Invite unlimited team members with org-wide or project-level access. Share dashboards publicly or lock them behind a password.
home.collaboration_features_integrations_desc: Forward events to your own systems or third-party tools. Connect OpenPanel to Slack, your data warehouse, or any webhook endpoint.
home.pricing_feature_6: Yes, we have no limits or hidden costs
home.faq_1_question: Does OpenPanel have a free tier?
home.faq_1_answer:
For our Cloud plan we offer a 30 days free trial, this is mostly for you to be able to try out OpenPanel before committing to a paid plan. OpenPanel is also open-source and you can self-host it for free!

Why does OpenPanel not have a free tier? We want to make sure that OpenPanel is used by people who are serious about using it. We also need to invest time and resources to maintain the platform and provide support to our users.
home.faq_2_answer:
Yes! With OpenPanel, you get unlimited websites/apps, unlimited users, unlimited dashboards, unlimited charts, and unlimited tracked profiles.

The only limit is the number of events you track per month, which you choose based on your needs.
home.faq_3_question: What is the difference between web and product analytics?
home.faq_3_answer:
Web analytics focuses on website traffic, page views, and visitor behavior. Product analytics goes deeper, tracking user interactions, events, and product usage patterns.

OpenPanel combines both, giving you a complete view of your users and product performance.
home.faq_4_question: Do I need to modify my code to use OpenPanel?
home.faq_4_answer:
OpenPanel offers multiple SDKs and integration options. For most frameworks, you can get started with just a few lines of code.

We provide SDKs for React, Next.js, Vue, Astro, and many more. Check our documentation for your specific framework.
home.faq_5_answer:
Yes! OpenPanel is designed with privacy in mind. We use cookie-less tracking, don't collect personal data without consent, and give you full control over your data.

You can self-host to ensure complete data sovereignty.
home.faq_6_question: How does OpenPanel compare to other analytics tools?
home.faq_6_answer: We have a dedicated compare page where you can see how OpenPanel compares to other analytics tools. You can find it [here](/compare). You can also read our comprehensive guide on the [best open source web analytics tools](/articles/self-hosted-web-analytics).
home.faq_7_question: Is OpenPanel a good Mixpanel alternative?
home.faq_7_answer:
Yes — OpenPanel covers the core features most teams use in Mixpanel: event tracking, funnels, retention, cohorts, and user profiles. The key differences are pricing, privacy, and self-hosting.

OpenPanel starts at $2.50/month and can be self-hosted for free, while Mixpanel is cloud-only and scales to hundreds or thousands per month. OpenPanel is also cookie-free by default with EU-only hosting, so no consent banners required — something Mixpanel can't offer.

See the full [OpenPanel vs Mixpanel comparison](/compare/mixpanel-alternative) for a side-by-side breakdown.
home.faq_8_question: How does OpenPanel compare to Plausible?
home.faq_8_answer:
OpenPanel shares Plausible's privacy-first approach and simplicity, but adds powerful product analytics capabilities.

While Plausible focuses on web analytics, OpenPanel combines both web and product analytics in one platform.
home.faq_9_question: How does OpenPanel compare to Google Analytics?
home.faq_9_answer:
OpenPanel is a privacy-first alternative to Google Analytics. Unlike GA, we don't use cookies, respect user privacy, and give you full control over your data.

Plus, you get product analytics features that GA doesn't offer.
home.faq_10_answer:
Absolutely! You own your data and can export it anytime. We have API endpoints to get all raw data that we have access to.

If you self-host, you have direct access and own all your data. For our cloud service, you can always reach out to us if you want a database dump of all your data—perfect if you want to move from cloud to self-hosting.

We have no lock-in whatsoever.
home.faq_11_question: What kind of support do you offer?
home.faq_11_answer:
We offer support through our documentation, GitHub issues, and Discord community. For paid plans, we provide email support.

Our team is committed to helping you succeed with OpenPanel.
home.cta_title_line_1: Ready to understand your users better?

pages.tools_url_checker_description: Analyze any URL for SEO, social, technical, and security data
pages.tools_ip_lookup_description: Find your IP address and geolocation data
pages.tools_url_checker_description_long: Analyze any website for SEO, social media, technical, and security information. Get comprehensive insights about any URL.
pages.tools_url_checker_body_title: Free URL Checker and Website Analysis Tool
pages.tools_url_checker_body_description: Analyze any URL for SEO issues, security problems, and performance bottlenecks. This tool checks meta tags, social previews, security headers, and server configuration in seconds.
pages.tools_url_checker_section_1_title: What does this URL checker analyze?
pages.tools_url_checker_section_1_description: Enter any URL to inspect SEO health, social sharing previews, response times, server location, SSL certificate status, and key security headers.
pages.tools_url_checker_section_2_description: The tool checks title tags, meta descriptions, H1 tags, canonical URLs, robots meta tags, and robots.txt status so you can catch indexing and click-through issues.
pages.tools_url_checker_section_3_description: Open Graph and Twitter Card tags control how your links appear on social platforms. Missing tags often lead to poor previews.
pages.tools_url_checker_section_4_description: Results break down DNS lookup, connection, TLS handshake, TTFB, and total response time so you can tell whether slowness comes from the network, server, or configuration.
pages.tools_url_checker_section_5_description: The tool checks SSL certificates, CSP, X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options, and HSTS headers.
pages.tools_url_checker_section_6_description: Enter any URL, click check, wait a few seconds, then review the SEO, Social, Technical, and Security tabs to fix missing items or warnings.
pages.tools_url_checker_faq_1_question: What's the difference between a URL checker and a site checker?
pages.tools_url_checker_faq_1_answer: A URL checker analyzes a single page. A full site checker crawls your entire website. This tool checks individual URLs, which is faster and useful for spot-checking specific pages.
pages.tools_url_checker_faq_2_question: How often should I check my URLs?
pages.tools_url_checker_faq_2_answer: Check after publishing new pages, making major updates, or changing hosting/CDN configuration. Key pages should be audited monthly.
pages.tools_url_checker_faq_3_answer: Under 200ms is usually good, 200-500ms is acceptable, and above 500ms should be investigated.
pages.tools_url_checker_faq_4_question: What security score should I aim for?
pages.tools_url_checker_faq_4_answer: 100% means all four security headers are present. Aim for at least 75%. CSP can be more complex, but the other headers are usually easy to configure.
pages.tools_url_checker_faq_5_answer: Yes. This tool works with any public URL and can help you understand competitor SEO, hosting, and security configuration.
pages.tools_url_checker_faq_6_question: Why is a page marked as not indexable?
pages.tools_url_checker_faq_6_answer: Common reasons include a noindex meta tag, robots.txt blocking crawlers, or a canonical pointing elsewhere. The SEO tab shows the specific cause.
pages.tools_url_checker_faq_7_answer: Yes, completely free. To keep it available, each IP can make up to 10 checks per minute.
pages.tools_url_checker_final_description: Enter any URL above to get instant SEO, performance, security, and social sharing insights before issues hurt rankings or user experience.
pages.tools_ip_lookup_description_long: Find detailed information about any IP address including geolocation, ISP, and network details.
pages.tools_ip_lookup_placeholder: Enter IP address or leave empty to detect yours
pages.tools_ip_lookup_body_title: Free IP Lookup Tool: Find Your IP Address and Geolocation
pages.tools_ip_lookup_body_description: Discover your IP address instantly and get detailed geolocation information including country, city, ISP, ASN, and network details. This tool uses the MaxMind GeoLite2 database for location data.
pages.tools_ip_lookup_section_1_description: An IP (Internet Protocol) address is a unique numerical identifier assigned to every device connected to a network, similar to a mailing address for your device on the internet. Common types include IPv4 and IPv6.
pages.tools_ip_lookup_section_2_description: IP geolocation estimates the approximate location of an IP address using routing information and regional IP allocation. It is usually accurate to the city level and reflects network infrastructure location rather than an exact physical address.
pages.tools_ip_lookup_section_3_title: What does the IP lookup result include?
pages.tools_ip_lookup_section_3_description: Results may include country, city, region, coordinates, ISP, ASN, organization, and hostname information to help you understand network origin and approximate location.
pages.tools_ip_lookup_section_4_description: IP lookup can be used for security checks, content localization, website analytics, compliance checks, network troubleshooting, and fraud prevention.
pages.tools_ip_lookup_section_5_description: An IP address can reveal approximate location and network information, but usually not your exact address or identity. Using a VPN or proxy changes the public IP shown to others.
pages.tools_ip_lookup_faq_1_answer: It is usually accurate to the city level, but this varies by region and provider. It shows network infrastructure location, not your exact physical address.
pages.tools_ip_lookup_faq_2_question: Can I hide my IP address?
pages.tools_ip_lookup_faq_2_answer: Yes. You can use a VPN or proxy service to hide your real IP and present the VPN or proxy server's address instead.
pages.tools_ip_lookup_faq_3_question: Why does an IP location look wrong?
pages.tools_ip_lookup_faq_3_answer: IP locations reflect the location of the operator's network equipment and may differ from your actual location. This is common with mobile networks and some ISPs.
pages.tools_ip_lookup_faq_4_answer: Yes, this IP lookup tool is completely free. To keep usage fair, each IP can make up to 20 requests per minute.
pages.tools_ip_lookup_faq_5_answer: ASN, or Autonomous System Number, is a unique identifier for a network operator's routing domain. It helps identify the organization controlling a given IP block.
pages.tools_ip_lookup_final_title: Start using the free IP lookup tool
pages.tools_ip_lookup_final_description: The page detects your IP automatically, and you can also enter any IPv4 or IPv6 address to instantly get network and geolocation details.
pages.supporter_description: Support OpenPanel and get exclusive perks like latest Docker images, prioritized support, and early access to new features.
pages.supporter_hero_description: Your support accelerates development, funds infrastructure, and helps us build features faster. Plus, you get exclusive perks and early access to everything we ship.
pages.supporter_why_description: We're not a big corporation – just a small team passionate about building something useful for developers. OpenPanel started because we believed analytics tools shouldn't be complicated or locked behind expensive enterprise subscriptions.
pages.supporter_funding_intro: When you become a supporter, you're directly funding:
pages.supporter_funding_outro: No corporate speak, no fancy promises – just honest work on making OpenPanel better for everyone. Every contribution, no matter the size, helps us stay independent and focused on what matters.

pages.open_source_hero_description: Track your users, understand adoption, and grow your project - all without cost. Get free analytics for your open source project with up to 2.5M events per month.

pages.open_source_why_body: We started OpenPanel because we believed analytics tools shouldn't be complicated or locked behind expensive enterprise subscriptions. As an open source project ourselves, we understand the challenges of building and growing a project without the resources of big corporations.
pages.open_source_ask_description: We keep it simple. Just a small way to help us grow and support more projects.
pages.open_source_ask_outro: That's it. No complicated requirements, no hidden fees, no catch. We just want to help open source projects succeed.

pages.supporter_cards_infrastructure_descripti: Keeping servers running, CI/CD pipelines, and development tools
pages.supporter_cards_independence_description: Staying focused on what matters: building a tool developers actually want

pages.supporter_cards_priority_support_descrip: Get help faster with priority support in our Discord community. Your questions get answered first.
pages.supporter_cards_feature_requests_descrip: Your ideas and feature requests get prioritized in our roadmap. Shape the future of OpenPanel.
pages.supporter_cards_discord_role_description: Special badge and recognition in our community. Show your support with pride.
pages.supporter_cards_open_source_description: Full transparency. Audit the code, contribute, fork it, or self-host without lock-in.
pages.supporter_cards_active247_description: Continuous improvements and updates. Your support enables faster development cycles.
pages.supporter_cards_self_hostable_descriptio: Deploy OpenPanel anywhere - your server, your cloud, or locally. Full flexibility.
pages.supporter_cards_early_access_description: Try new features before public release
pages.open_source_cards_events_description: More than enough for most open source projects. Track page views, user actions, and custom events without worrying about limits.
pages.open_source_cards_feature_access_descrip: Same powerful capabilities as paid plans. Funnels, retention analysis, custom dashboards, and real-time analytics.
pages.open_source_cards_team_description: Invite your entire contributor team. Collaborate with maintainers and core contributors on understanding your project's growth.
pages.open_source_cards_support_description: Dedicated help for open source maintainers. Get faster responses and priority assistance when you need it.
pages.open_source_cards_built_for_oss_descript: OpenPanel is open source. We know what it's like to build in the open.
pages.open_source_cards_no_barriers_descriptio: Analytics shouldn't be a barrier to understanding your users. We're removing that barrier.
pages.open_source_cards_giving_back_descriptio: We're giving back to the projects that inspire us and the community that supports us.
pages.open_source_cards_backlink_description: A simple link on your website or README helps others discover OpenPanel. It's a win-win for the community.
pages.open_source_cards_widget_description: Showcase your visitor count with our real-time analytics widget. It's completely optional but helps spread the word.
pages.open_source_cards_license_description: Your project must use an OSI-approved open source license (MIT, Apache, GPL, etc.)
pages.open_source_cards_repo_description: Your code must be publicly available on GitHub, GitLab, or similar platforms
pages.open_source_cards_active_description: Show evidence of active development and a growing community
pages.open_source_cards_non_commercial_descrip: The primary purpose should be non-commercial, though commercial OSS projects may be considered
pages.open_source_cards_info_description: Share your project URL, license type, and a brief description of what you're building
pages.open_source_cards_review_description: We'll evaluate your project and respond within a few business days
pages.open_source_faq_1_question: What counts as an open-source project?
pages.open_source_faq_1_answer: We consider any project with an OSI-approved open source license (MIT, Apache, GPL, BSD, etc.) that is publicly available and actively maintained. The project should have a non-commercial primary purpose, though we may consider commercial open source projects on a case-by-case basis.
pages.open_source_faq_2_question: What happens if I exceed 2.5M events per month?
pages.open_source_faq_2_answer: We understand that successful projects grow. If you consistently exceed 2.5M events, we'll reach out to discuss options. We're flexible and want to support your success. In most cases, we can work out a solution that works for both of us.
pages.open_source_faq_3_question: Can commercial open source projects apply?
pages.open_source_faq_3_answer: Yes, we consider commercial open source projects on a case-by-case basis. If your project is open source but has commercial offerings, please mention this in your application and we'll evaluate accordingly.
pages.open_source_faq_4_question: How long does the free access last?
pages.open_source_faq_4_answer: As long as your project remains eligible and active, your free access continues. We review projects periodically to ensure they still meet our criteria, but we're committed to supporting projects long-term.
pages.open_source_faq_5_question: Do I need to display the widget?
pages.open_source_faq_5_answer: No, displaying the widget is completely optional. We only require a backlink to OpenPanel on your website or README. The widget is just a nice way to showcase your analytics if you want to.
pages.open_source_faq_6_question: What if my project is very small or just starting?
pages.open_source_faq_6_answer: We welcome projects of all sizes! Whether you're just getting started or have a large community, if you meet our eligibility criteria, we'd love to help. Small projects often benefit the most from understanding their users early on.
pages.root_metadata_title: OpenPanel | An open-source alternative to Mixpanel
pages.root_metadata_description: OpenPanel is an open-source web and product analytics platform, an open-source alternative to Mixpanel with optional self-hosting.
pages.tools_url_checker_metadata_title: URL Checker - Free SEO, Open Graph & Meta Tag Inspector
pages.tools_url_checker_metadata_description: Inspect any URL's SEO, Open Graph, Twitter Cards, redirects, SSL, and tech stack. Free, instant, no signup. Built for developers and SEOs.
pages.tools_ip_lookup_metadata_title: What Is My IP? Free IP Address Lookup & Geolocation
pages.tools_ip_lookup_metadata_description: Find your IP address and location instantly. See city, country, ISP, ASN, and coordinates. Free, no signup, no tracking. Works for any IPv4 or IPv6 address.
